_meta.description: Shoulder Mobility Assessment System - Configurable Thresholds
_meta.note: All thresholds are pilot-tuned. Adjust based on patient population and clinical judgment.

detection_confidence.description: Minimum confidence for initial pose detection (new person appearing)
detection_confidence.clinical_note: Lower for poor lighting; higher to reduce false detections

tracking_confidence.description: Minimum confidence for frame-to-frame pose tracking
tracking_confidence.clinical_note: Lower for faster movement; higher for more reliable landmarks

reps_per_set.description: Target number of repetitions per set for automatic set-complete detection
reps_per_set.clinical_note: Typical frozen shoulder rehab: 10-15 reps. Set to 0 to disable auto-detection.

total_sets.description: Number of sets per exercise session
total_sets.clinical_note: Standard frozen shoulder protocol: 3 sets. Adjust for patient tolerance.

smoothing_window.description: Moving average window size for angle smoothing in rep segmentation
smoothing_window.clinical_note: Smaller = more responsive; larger = more stable. 5 @ 30 FPS ≈ 0.17 sec.

rom_classification.description: Minimum peak angle to classify a rep as 'correct' ROM
rom_classification.research_basis: Gill et al. (2020) - mean abduction ~150° in healthy adults (N=2404)
rom_classification.clinical_note: Lower for patients with severe adhesive capsulitis; raise for advanced rehab stages

start_threshold.description: Angle to enter 'in-rep' state for abduction exercise
start_threshold.research_basis: Frontal view baseline ~21-25° due to natural arm hang angle
start_threshold.clinical_note: Higher than flexion due to frontal view baseline offset

end_threshold.research_basis: Canny (1986) hysteresis principle for noise reduction
end_threshold.clinical_note: Must be lower than start_threshold to prevent oscillation

start_threshold.description: Angle to enter 'in-rep' state for flexion exercise
start_threshold.research_basis: Lateral view baseline ~0-8° (arm aligns with trunk vector)
start_threshold.clinical_note: Lower than abduction due to lateral view baseline

end_threshold.research_basis: Canny (1986) hysteresis principle for noise reduction
end_threshold.clinical_note: Must be lower than start_threshold to prevent oscillation

min_peak_distance.description: Minimum frames between peaks to prevent double-counting
min_peak_distance.research_basis: ~0.5 sec @ 30 FPS; Coates & Wahlström (2023) turning-point guards
min_peak_distance.clinical_note: Increase if patients move very slowly

min_peak_prominence.description: Minimum peak height above start threshold for valid rep
min_peak_prominence.research_basis: Hsu et al. (2023) viewpoint-invariant rep counting
min_peak_prominence.clinical_note: Lower for patients with very limited ROM

trunk_lean.description: Maximum trunk deviation from vertical before flagging compensation
trunk_lean.research_basis: Nakatsuchi et al. 2021, Christensen et al. 2021 (analogous movements)
trunk_lean.clinical_note: Increase for patients with postural issues unrelated to compensation

shoulder_hiking.description: Shoulder asymmetry ratio threshold (affected vs unaffected side) — thesis §3.6.4: 45%
shoulder_hiking.research_basis: Thesis §3.6.4: 45% shoulder asymmetry. Pilot-tuned from Mohamed et al. 2020.
shoulder_hiking.clinical_note: Higher threshold = fewer false positives but may miss subtle hiking

trunk_lean_persistence.description: Fraction of rep frames where trunk lean must be detected to flag the rep (persistence filter)
trunk_lean_persistence.clinical_note: Higher = requires more sustained lean to flag (fewer false positives). Lower = more sensitive.

shoulder_hiking_persistence.description: Fraction of rep frames where shoulder hiking must be detected to flag the rep (persistence filter)
shoulder_hiking_persistence.clinical_note: Higher = requires more sustained hiking to flag (fewer false positives). Lower = more sensitive.

cwema_alpha_base.description: Base EMA smoothing factor (α) for confidence-weighted exponential moving average
cwema_alpha_base.research_basis: Thesis computational contribution - CW-EMA landmark smoothing
cwema_alpha_base.clinical_note: Lower = more smoothing (lag); higher = more responsive (noise). 0.4 typical for 30 FPS video.

cwema_c_floor.description: Minimum confidence floor for EMA alpha computation
cwema_c_floor.research_basis: Thesis - prevents effective alpha from collapsing to near-zero when confidence momentarily dips
cwema_c_floor.clinical_note: Prevents loss of responsiveness during low-confidence frames. Typical: 5% of max confidence.

bone_length_beta.description: Bone-Length Constancy (BLC) geometric foreshortening threshold: frames where L_t / L_baseline < beta are flagged as unreliable (fixed design constant; see loso_cv_tuning frozen_params for justification)
bone_length_beta.research_basis: Thesis - BLC validates joints maintain anatomically consistent distances
bone_length_beta.clinical_note: Higher penalty = more aggressive unscorable rep flagging when anatomy violated.

bone_length_segment.description: MediaPipe segment to use as baseline bone length (right arm when affected_side='Left')
bone_length_segment.research_basis: Thesis - uses anthropometric constancy; ~0.4-0.6 of torso in FS demographics
bone_length_segment.clinical_note: Should be upper limb segment for shoulder exercises. Swapped based on affected_side.

sma_window_size.description: Simple moving average window size for SMA pipeline (comparison baseline)
sma_window_size.research_basis: Thesis - enables ablation study with SMA as traditional smoothing comparison
sma_window_size.clinical_note: Smaller = less lag; larger = more stable. 5 @ 30 FPS ≈ 0.17 sec.

unscorable_frame_pct.description: Threshold for flagging rep as unscorable if >N% of frames violate BLC constraint
unscorable_frame_pct.research_basis: Thesis - automates detection of reps with tracking instability or occlusion
unscorable_frame_pct.clinical_note: Lower = more strict; higher = more forgiving. 20% typical (allow brief occlusions).

duration_absolute_cap.description: Absolute maximum duration for a valid rep (~8 seconds at 30 FPS)
duration_absolute_cap.research_basis: Thesis §3.7 - prevents inclusion of extremely slow reps with tracking artifacts
duration_absolute_cap.clinical_note: Set to 240 frames (~8 sec) for 30 FPS video. Scales appropriately with frame rate. Works with relative cap (3× baseline).

medium_threshold.description: ROM decline or duration increase to trigger 'Medium' fatigue
medium_threshold.clinical_note: Lower = more sensitive (earlier warnings); higher = fewer interruptions

high_threshold.description: ROM decline or duration increase to trigger 'High' fatigue

severe_threshold.description: ROM decline to trigger stop rule (session termination recommendation)
severe_threshold.clinical_note: Critical safety threshold - adjust with caution

window_size.description: Sliding window of valid reps for fatigue metric calculation
window_size.research_basis: Balance between responsiveness and noise reduction
window_size.clinical_note: Smaller = more responsive; larger = more stable

cooldown_reps.description: Minimum reps after break before another break can be triggered
cooldown_reps.research_basis: Prevents break prompt spam; reduced to 2 for 10-rep evaluation sets
cooldown_reps.clinical_note: Set to 2 to allow a second fatigue trigger within a 10-rep set if needed

medium.description: Recommended break duration for Medium fatigue

high.description: Recommended break duration for High fatigue

use_fuzzy_inference.description: Enables Mamdani fuzzy inference for performance deterioration scoring and micro-break prompting. When false, the system uses the threshold-based fallback mode.
use_fuzzy_inference.research_basis: Thesis §3.7 - Mamdani fuzzy inference is the default module for deterioration scoring, with threshold-based mode retained as a fallback for comparison and troubleshooting.
use_fuzzy_inference.clinical_note: Both modes produce Low/Medium/High deterioration levels. Set to false only when running threshold-based fallback comparisons or troubleshooting.

compensation_escalation.description: Number of reps with compensation flags in the window before triggering a form cue
compensation_escalation.clinical_note: Lower = more sensitive form warnings (catch subtle compensation earlier); higher = only flag clear/repeated compensation

min_valid_reps_for_trigger.description: Minimum valid reps in window before any fatigue trigger is allowed (prevents early false alarms)
min_valid_reps_for_trigger.clinical_note: Raise to 4-5 if early-session false fatigue triggers are observed; lower to 2 for patients with very short sets

consecutive_windows_required.description: Number of consecutive qualifying fatigue windows required before triggering a break (persistence filter)
consecutive_windows_required.clinical_note: 1 = trigger on first sign of fatigue (more sensitive); 2 = require two reps in a row (default, fewer false triggers)

fuzzy_threshold_low.description: Fuzzy deterioration score above which fatigue is classified as Medium (below = Low)
fuzzy_threshold_low.clinical_note: Lower = classify Medium fatigue earlier (more cautious); higher = require stronger signal before escalating

fuzzy_threshold_high.description: Fuzzy deterioration score above which fatigue is classified as High (below threshold_low = Low)
fuzzy_threshold_high.clinical_note: Must be greater than fuzzy_threshold_low. Lower = escalate to High sooner; higher = reserve High for clear fatigue only

correct_rom_good_pct.description: Minimum correct ROM percentage to label session as 'Good' in reports
correct_rom_good_pct.clinical_note: Lower for severe cases; higher for advanced rehab stages

avg_peak_good_angle.description: Minimum average peak angle to label session performance as 'Good'
avg_peak_good_angle.clinical_note: Matches ROM classification threshold by default

compensation_acceptable_pct.description: Maximum fraction of reps with compensation before labeling as 'Frequent'
compensation_acceptable_pct.clinical_note: 20% default: up to 2 of 10 reps with compensation is acceptable

high_fatigue_significant_count.description: Number of high-fatigue events before labeling as 'Significant' in reports

high_compensation_rate_pct.description: Compensation rate threshold for CSV anomaly flag (>50% of reps)
high_compensation_rate_pct.clinical_note: Flags sessions with unusually high compensation for review

calibration_duration_sec.description: Duration of the calibration countdown window
calibration_duration_sec.clinical_note: 10 seconds provides adequate stabilization time for frozen-shoulder patients with limited mobility and postural instability.

calibration_jitter_threshold_px.description: Maximum acceptable standard deviation of landmark position during calibration
calibration_jitter_threshold_px.clinical_note: Default 40px. Lower = stricter stability requirement. Higher = more forgiving for natural sway and high-resolution webcams.
